summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a 1978 blow‑out with scorching Scarlet > Fire, monster Music Never Stopped and a killer first set.
review: The recording is a very good sound‑board source that stays clear and balanced throughout, only marred by an out‑of‑tune Werewolves encore and one odd drum “space” moment. Musically the show is a quintessential 1978 performance – a tight, high‑energy first set highlighted by Ramble On Rose, Cassidy and Music Never Stopped, followed by a second‑set firestorm of Scarlet > Fire, Terrapin, Passenger and Black Peter that many reviewers call “mind‑blowing.”

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (many 5‑star) and note good to excellent soundboard quality; only minor criticisms of the encore and a quirky drum segment keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear, crisp mix with solid balance across band; occasional odd drum noise noted but overall very listenable. The Werewolves encore is out‑of‑tune, which some reviewers call a blemish.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong, inspired first set (Ramblin' Rose, Cassidy, Music Never Stopped) feeds into a blistering second set where Scarlet > Fire rolls straight into Terrapin and Passenger. The band clicks tightly throughout, with high‑energy transitions and a few memorable vocal harmonies from Donna.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring solos, fiery lead work on Scarlet/Fire and competitive interplay with Bobby; described as “soulful” and “taking it to church.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for a “blatant kick‑ass bass” performance that fuels the band’s drive.
band_member_comments.Bob: Celebrated for aggressive rhythm/slides, dual‑lead moments with Jerry, and overall “finesse” that lifts many songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is noted for beautiful piano outro on Cassidy, strong moments in the second set, and occasional “strong moments” despite some criticism of his 1978 consistency.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Rhythm Devils are praised for energetic playing; one reviewer mentions a mysterious “space” or animal‑like sound during the drums.
